title: horse mode basement light
excerpt: The frontsite stays polished. This room keeps the table messy, the light on, and the strange files within reach.
body: Playground archive means the entry can stay unfinished without feeling abandoned.
This is the layer for sketches, evidence, accidents, half-systems, and stray buttons that should not need a full project page to exist.

title: discarded symbol study
excerpt: A recovered mark from the maybe-final folder, kept because the wrong version sometimes has better weather.
body: Some entries can be image-first: a scan, a screenshot, a texture, a failed icon, a thing that keeps looking back.
The note can be short. The archive should not punish smallness.

title: diet coke ledger
excerpt: A tiny accounting system for emotional truth, unclear medicine, and extremely real line items.
body: Scratch entries can point outward to existing rooms. The archive becomes a switchboard, not just a list.
A single entry can be a thumbnail, a caption, and a door.

title: save as ritual
excerpt: A place to keep screenshots, old UI feelings, and the private ceremony of naming a file before it becomes real.
body: This format is useful for process notes: what the object is, why it was kept, what it might become.
The entry does not need closure. It needs coordinates.
